name: Pumpkin Cake with Seafoam Frosting
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Sifted cake flour (sift before measuring)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
Grease and flour three 8-by-1 1/2-inch layer-cake pans.
Sift together the cake flour, baking powder, soda, salt, cinnamon, and cloves onto waxed paper.
2. In a 2-cup measuring cup, combine pumpkin and milk, mixing well.
Add granulated sugar, brown sugar, butter, eggs, and vanilla extract to a large bowl of electric mixer.
Beat at high speed about 5 minutes, scraping the side of the bowl with a rubber scraper occasionally.
3. At low speed, beat in the flour mixture (in fourths) alternately with pumpkin mixture (in thirds).
Beat just until smooth for 1 minute. Pour batter into pans and bake for 30 to 35 minutes,
or until the surface springs back when gently pressed with a fingertip.
Cool in the pans for 10 minutes.
4. Remove from pans; cool completely on a wire rack.
Make Frosting: In the top of a double boiler, combine egg whites, light-brown sugar (packed), corn syrup, and water.
Beat until combined.
5. Cook over rapidly boiling water, beating constantly, about 7 minutes,
or until stiff peaks form when the beater is slowly raised.
Remove from boiling water. Add vanilla extract; continue beating until the frosting is thick enough to spread (about 2 minutes).
6. Place a layer on a plate, top side down; spread with about 1 cup of frosting and sprinkle with chopped walnuts.
Repeat with second layer, top side down. Frost the cake with the remaining frosting swirling decoratively.
Garnish the edge with walnut halves. Refrigerate until serving.
Serves 12
Recipe from: McCall's, October 1984
